summary: Import-time remote code execution via GitHub Gist eval
details: On require/import, index.js fetches JavaScript from a personal GitHub Gist (gist.githubusercontent.com/metabrainai000/198a0bbec7a6018e9250615d26e37b90/raw/.../script.js) and passes the response body directly to eval(). The remote content is unpinned, mutable by the Gist owner, and unrelated to any publisher infrastructure — any consumer who imports the package executes arbitrary attacker-controlled JavaScript with the privileges of the importing process. The package is also a lure: it is published under a scope mimicking the SQLite ecosystem ('@sqlite-node') with the name 'createsql' and a description claiming 'A SQLite Toolkit for node', yet ships no SQL or SQLite code — only the fetch+eval dropper. The declared repository URL points to an unrelated 'array-utl_nodelump' repo, further indicating the SQLite branding is cover.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
